segments.text: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
How long wilt thou speak these things? and how long shall the words of thy mouth be like a strong wind?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: How long wilt thou speak these things? and how long shall the words of thy mouth be like a strong wind?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Doth God pervert judgment? or doth the Almighty pervert justice?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: If thy children have sinned against him, and he have cast them away for their transgression;
segments.speaker: Bildad

footnotes: for…: Heb. in the hand of their transgression

segments.text: If thou wouldest seek unto God betimes, and make thy supplication to the Almighty;
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: If thou wert pure and upright; surely now he would awake for thee, and make the habitation of thy righteousness prosperous.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Though thy beginning was small, yet thy latter end should greatly increase.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: For enquire, I pray thee, of the former age, and prepare thyself to the search of their fathers:
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: (For we are but of yesterday, and know nothing, because our days upon earth are a shadow:)
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Shall not they teach thee, and tell thee, and utter words out of their heart?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Can the rush grow up without mire? can the flag grow without water?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Whilst it is yet in his greenness, and not cut down, it withereth before any other herb.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: So are the paths of all that forget God; and the hypocrite's hope shall perish:
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Whose hope shall be cut off, and whose trust shall be a spider's web.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: He shall lean upon his house, but it shall not stand: he shall hold it fast, but it shall not endure.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: He is green before the sun, and his branch shooteth forth in his garden.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: His roots are wrapped about the heap, and seeth the place of stones.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: If he destroy him from his place, then it shall deny him, saying, I have not seen thee.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Behold, this is the joy of his way, and out of the earth shall others grow.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Behold, God will not cast away a perfect man, neither will he help the evil doers:
segments.speaker: Bildad

footnotes: help…: Heb. take the ungodly by the hand; come: Heb. shall not be

segments.text: Till he fill thy mouth with laughing, and thy lips with rejoicing.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: They that hate thee shall be clothed with shame; and the dwelling place of the wicked shall come to nought.
segments.speaker: Bildad

footnotes: shall come…: Heb. shall not be
